ApplicationLibrariesReferenceGuide Documentation

Tilera®  provides a variety of software libraries to help accelerate high-performance application development on the Tile Processor™. This document provides an API reference for most of the libraries that can be used by userspace applications. This documentation is distinct from the System Programmer's Guide (UG509), which describes how to customize the Tile Processor's system software environment.

This guide documents the following libraries:

  • Tilera Multicore Components (TMC™) Library : Utility routines for parallel program development.

  • Tile Architecture Headers : Chip-specific routines that can be used at any layer of the system software stack.

  • gxio Low-Level IO Device Control API : Allocate and control IO devices directly from userspace.

  • gxcr User-Level Crypto Acceleration API : Perform hardware-accelerated cryptographic and packet processing operations.

  • gxpci PCIe and StreamIO Data Transfer API : Perform data transfer operations among TILE-Gx PCIe ports and between TILE-Gx PCIe endpoint ports and an x86 host directly from userspace.

Libraries that are only useful in certain, limited circumstances are listed in Miscellaneous Components.

All other trademarks and/or registered trademarks are the property of their respective owners.

Third-party software: The Tilera IDE makes use of the BeanShell scripting library. Source code for the BeanShell library can be found at the BeanShell website (http://www.beanshell.org/developer.html).

The following is a trademark of Marvell Semiconductor, Inc.: Distributed Switching Architecture (DSA).
